attributes.action: Align Barnet Council HMO Room Size Standards with National Guidelines
attributes.additional_details: Barnet’s 10.2sqm rule reduces compliant HMO rooms, cutting supply of affordable housing and forcing landlords to raise rents on remaining rooms. It restricts lower-cost options for tenants, particularly those on limited incomes. The policy also conflicts with national standards, creating confusion, inefficiency, and potential legal disputes. The 6.51sqm limit already ensures safety; aligning with it would boost supply, improve affordability, and maintain protections.
attributes.background:
Revert to the national minimum room size of 6.51 sqm where all other HMO conditions are met.

Introduce safeguards against exploitation, including fair or proportionate pricing for smaller rooms.

Maintain enforcement of all other HMO standards to protect tenants.

rejection.details:
We can't accept your petition because the UK Government and Parliament aren't responsible for the issue you raise. This is the responsibility of your local council. 

You could raise your issue with a local councillor who represents you. You can use this page to find out who your local councillors are, and how to contact them: https://www.gov.uk/find-your-local-councillors
